sections.paragraph: αἰτία, ας, ἡ [assoc. w. αἰτέω; ‘guilt, blame’, also ‘cause’] ‘the basis for something’, reason, cause, circumstance Mt 19:3; Luke 8:47; 2 Tim 1:6; Hb 2:11. As legal term: cause/case (for indictment or punishment) = crime Mt 27:37. In J 18:38 Pilate declares that he has no cause for an indictment, that is, he has no case = no indication of crime; sim. Ac 25:18. A transition to such legal usage can be seen in Mt 19:10: ‘if such is the caseinvolving a man and his wife’.
